content_en:
Abu Hurairah reported that the Messenger of Allah # said, “Whoever it pleases that Allah should give him wine to drink in the Hereafter, then let him forsake it in the world. And whomever it pleases that Allah should clothe him in silk in the Hereafter, then let him forsake it in the world. The rivers of Paradise gush forth from underneath hills ~ or mountains ~ of musk (the best kind of perfume). Were the jewels (ornaments) of the lowest dweller of Paradise to be compared to the jewels (ornaments) of all the inhabitants of the world, the jewels (ornaments) of the lowest dweller of Paradise would be superior to the jewels (ornaments) of all inhabitants of the world (put together).” (Related by Tbn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

By way of Abu Mu’aawiyah, ‘Abdullah # said, “The rivers of Paradise gush forth from musk (the best kind of perfume).” This is more authentic as a saying of ‘Abdullah than it is as a saying of the Prophet #84.

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 645

A Description Of Al-Kauthar, The Most Famous of The Rivers Of Paradise. May Allah 3¢ Give Us Drink From It, By His Grace And Generosity

“Verily, We have granted you (O Muhammad 38) Al-Kauthar (a

river in Paradise). Therefore turn in prayer to your Lord and

sacrifice (to Him only). For he who hates you (O Muhammad 2%),

he will be cut of (from posterity and every good thing in this world

646 Book of ‘fhe End: Great ‘rials and ‘Iribulations

In another narration, Anas reported that the Messenger of Allah #6 said, “I have been given Al-Kauthar; it is a river that flows on the surface of the ground; its two sides are domes of pearls, without being roofed. I struck my hand on its soil, and its soil was musk (the best kind of perfume) Adhfar (pure, unmixed), and its pebbles are pearls.” (Ahmad)

Anas ibn Maalik 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah 2% was asked about the Kauthar, and he 2% said, “It is river in Paradise which Allah gave me. Its soil is musk (the best kind of perfume). Its water is whiter than milk and sweeter than honey. Birds, whose necks are like the necks of Al-Jazoor (camels that are ready to be slaughtered), come to drink from it” Abu Bakr & said, “O Messenger of Allah, they (the birds) are soft?” He #% said, “Eating them is softer than them.” (Ahmad)

Hudhaifah . related that the Messenger of Allah 38 said, “Verily, in Paradise there are birds that are like Al-Bukhaatee (camels that have long necks).” Abu Bakr % said, “They are soft, O Messenger of Allah?” He #8 said, “The one who eats them is softer than them, and you are from those who will eat them, O Abu Bakr.” (Related by AlHaakim) Al-Haakim then related it through Qataadah in Mursal form.

The Narration Of Ibn “Umar

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 647

narration of this hadith, and the latter said, “Hasan sahih.”

The Narration Of Ibn ‘Abbaas

Sa’eed ibn Jubair related that Ibn ‘Abbaas said about the Kauthar, “It is the goodness that Allah. gave him (ie, gave to the Prophet #8). (Bukhaaree)

Tbn Bishr said, “I said to Sa’eed ibn Jubair, ‘Some people claim that it is a river in Paradise.” Sa’eed said, “The River that is in Paradise is from the goodness that Allah gave to him (.¢., to the Prophet ##).”” Ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Al-Kauthar is a river in Paradise; its two sides are gold and silver, and it flows on rubies and pearls. Its water is whiter than snow and sweeter than honey.” (Related by Ibn Jareer) Al’Aoufee related the same from Tbn ‘Abbaas +

The Narration Of ‘Aishah %

Abu ‘Ubaidah #: said, “I asked ‘Aishah % about the saying of Allah

“Verily, We have granted you (O Muhammad #) Al-Kauthar (a river in Paradise).'"") She % said, “Al-Kauthar is a river which your Prophet #3 was given. Its two shores are hollow pearls, and its vessels are, in number, like the stars.” (Bukhaaree) And ‘Aishah % said, “It is a river in Paradise. When one puts his fingers in his ears, he hears the flowing sound of that river.” In another narration, ‘Aishah % said, “Whoever would love to hear the flowing sound of the water of Al-Kauthar, then he will not hear it with his eyes, but rather its sound is like the sound that is heard when a person puts two of his fingers in his ears.” (Related by Ibn Jareer)

A Mention Of The Al-Baidakh River In Paradise

‘Anas 4 said, “The Messenger of Allah 2 liked the good dream, and maybe he would say, ‘Did any of you see a dream?’... A woman.

648 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

(once) came and said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, | saw what was like me entering Paradise, and I heard a sound, for which the people of Paradise cried. I looked, and I saw that so-and-so son of so-and-so was brought, and so-and-so-son of so-and-so, until I counted 12 men,’ It happened that the Messenger of Allah #% had sent a group on a mission just before that. ‘They were brought, and upon them were dirty garments, and the veins around their necks were dripping blood. It was said: take them to Al-Baidakh — or the river of Al-Baidakh. They immersed themselves in it, and when they came out, their faces were like the moon on the night of the full moon. Then chairs of gold were brought and they sat on them. Then a tray... was brought; in it were unripe dates, and they ate from them. They did not change its sides except that they ate from the fruit that they wanted, and I ate with them,’ The herald of that group (that went on a mission) came and said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, such and such were the matters that we went through. So and so was afflicted (ie., killed), until he counted the same twelve that the woman enumerated. The Messenger of Allah #¥ said, ‘Bring the ‘woman to me.’ She came and he #& said, ‘Give an account of your dream to this one.’ She gave an account of it, and he said, ‘It is as she said, O Messenger of Allah.” (Atmad)

A Shining River At The Gate Of Paradise

Ibn ‘Abbaas . related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “The martyrs are on Baarig (literally, shining) river at the gate of Paradise, ina green dome, Their sustenance comes out to them in the morning and in the evening.” (Ahmad)

‘The following is in the hadith of Al-Israa (the night journey), when Sidratul-Muntahaa is being mentioned: “And two internal (or hidden) rivers are coming out of its source, and two external tivers. The two internal rivers are in Paradise, and the external ones are the Nile and the Euphrates.”

Abu Buraizah . reported that the Messenger of Allah #¢ said, “Saihaan, Jaihaan, the Euphrates, and the Nile — these are all from the rivers of Paradise.” Ahmad and Muslim related this, and the

Jbn ‘Abbaas # reported that the Prophet 4 said, “Allah sent down

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 649

five rivers from Paradise: Saihoon, and it is the tiver of India; Jaihoon, and it is the river of Balkh; Dijlah and the Euphrates, and they are the rivers of Iraq; and the Nile, which is the river of Egypt. Allah a sent them down from one source, from the sources of Paradise — from the lowest of their levels, on the wings of Jibreel. He deposited them in the mountains and made them flow in the earth. And He placed many benefits in them for the people, in the different aspects of their livelihood. And that is the saying of Allah

“And we sent down from the sky water (rain) in (due) measure, and

We gave it lodging in the eartl at} “When Yajooj and Majooj come out, Allah will send Jibreel, and he will raise the following from the earth: the Qus’an AlAdheem; all knowledge; the black stone, from the corner of the House (Ka’bah), at the Station of Tbraaheem; the Taaboot (Ark) of Moosa, with that which is in it; and these five rivers. All of that will be raised to the sky, and that is the saying of Allah #8:
content_id:
Abu Hurairah melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Barang siapa yang menyenangkannya untuk Allah memberinya minum dengan anggur di akhirat, maka hendaklah dia meninggalkannya di dunia. Dan barang siapa yang menyenangkannya untuk Allah mengenakan pakaian sutra kepadanya di akhirat, maka hendaklah dia meninggalkannya di dunia. Sungai-sungai Surga mengalir dari bawah bukit-bukit — atau gunung-gunung — kesturi (jenis parfum terbaik). Seandainya perhiasan (ornamen) penghuni Surga dengan peringkat terendah dibandingkan dengan perhiasan (ornamen) semua penduduk dunia, perhiasan (ornamen) penghuni Surga dengan peringkat terendah akan lebih unggul dari perhiasan (ornamen) semua penduduk dunia (digabungkan)." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Melalui Abu Mu'aawiyah, 'Abdullah رضي الله عنه berkata, "Sungai-sungai Surga mengalir dari kesturi (jenis parfum terbaik)." Ini lebih sahih sebagai perkataan 'Abdullah daripada sebagai sabda Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم.

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 645

Gambaran tentang Al-Kautsar, Sungai Paling Terkenal di Surga. Semoga Allah عز وجل Memberi Kami Minum Darinya, dengan Karunia dan Kemurahan-Nya

"Sesungguhnya, Kami telah menganugerahkan kepadamu (wahai Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) Al-Kautsar (sebuah sungai di Surga). Maka shalatlah kepada Tuhanmu dan berkurbanlah (hanya untuk-Nya). Sesungguhnya, orang yang membencimu (wahai Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), dialah yang terputus (dari keturunan dan setiap kebaikan di dunia ini.

646 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Dalam riwayat lain, Anas melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Aku telah diberikan Al-Kautsar; itu adalah sungai yang mengalir di permukaan tanah; kedua sisinya adalah kubah-kubah dari mutiara, tanpa beratap. Aku memukulkan tanganku ke tanahnya, dan tanahnya adalah kesturi (jenis parfum terbaik) Adzfar (murni, tidak tercampur), dan kerikil-kerikilnya adalah mutiara." (Ahmad)

Anas ibn Maalik رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم ditanya tentang Al-Kautsar, dan beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Itu adalah sungai di Surga yang Allah berikan kepadaku. Tanahnya adalah kesturi (jenis parfum terbaik). Airnya lebih putih dari susu dan lebih manis dari madu. Burung-burung, yang lehernya seperti leher Al-Jazoor (unta-unta yang siap disembelih), datang untuk minum darinya." Abu Bakr رضي الله عنه berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, apakah mereka (burung-burung) lembut?" Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Memakan mereka lebih lembut dari mereka." (Ahmad)

Hudzaifah رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, di Surga ada burung-burung yang seperti Al-Bukhaatee (unta-unta yang berleher panjang)." Abu Bakr رضي الله عنه berkata, "Mereka lembut, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Orang yang memakannya lebih lembut dari mereka, dan kamu termasuk mereka yang akan memakannya, wahai Abu Bakr." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haakim) Al-Haakim kemudian meriwayatkannya melalui Qataadah dalam bentuk Mursal.

Riwayat Ibnu 'Umar

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 647

narasi hadis ini, dan yang terakhir berkata, "Hasan sahih."

Riwayat Ibnu 'Abbaas

Sa'eed ibn Jubair meriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu 'Abbaas berkata tentang Al-Kautsar, "Itu adalah kebaikan yang Allah berikan kepadanya (yaitu diberikan kepada Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم)." (Bukhari)

Ibnu Bisyr berkata, "Aku berkata kepada Sa'eed ibn Jubair, 'Sebagian orang mengklaim bahwa itu adalah sungai di Surga.' Sa'eed berkata, 'Sungai yang ada di Surga adalah dari kebaikan yang Allah berikan kepadanya (yaitu kepada Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم).'" Ibnu 'Abbaas berkata, "Al-Kautsar adalah sungai di Surga; kedua sisinya adalah emas dan perak, dan ia mengalir di atas rubi dan mutiara. Airnya lebih putih dari salju dan lebih manis dari madu." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Jareer) Al-'Aufee meriwayatkan hal yang sama dari Ibnu 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه.

Riwayat 'Aisyah رضي الله عنها

Abu 'Ubaidah رضي الله عنه berkata, "Aku bertanya kepada 'Aisyah رضي الله عنها tentang firman Allah عز وجل:

'Sesungguhnya, Kami telah menganugerahkan kepadamu (wahai Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) Al-Kautsar (sebuah sungai di Surga).'" Dia رضي الله عنها berkata, "Al-Kautsar adalah sungai yang diberikan Allah kepada Nabimu صلى الله عليه وسلم. Kedua tepiannya adalah mutiara yang berongga, dan wadah-wadahnya jumlahnya seperti bintang-bintang." (Bukhari) Dan 'Aisyah رضي الله عنها berkata, "Itu adalah sungai di Surga. Ketika seseorang memasukkan jarinya ke dalam telinganya, dia mendengar suara gemericik sungai itu." Dalam riwayat lain, 'Aisyah رضي الله عنها berkata, "Barang siapa ingin mendengar suara gemericik air Al-Kautsar, maka dia tidak akan mendengarnya dengan matanya, melainkan suaranya seperti suara yang terdengar ketika seseorang memasukkan dua jarinya ke dalam telinganya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Jareer)

Penyebutan tentang Sungai Al-Baidakh di Surga

Anas رضي الله عنه berkata, "Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم menyukai mimpi yang baik, dan kadang-kadang beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم akan berkata, 'Apakah ada di antara kalian yang melihat mimpi?'... Seorang wanita

648 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

(suatu ketika) datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku melihat seolah-olah aku masuk ke Surga, dan aku mendengar sebuah suara, yang membuat penghuni Surga menangis. Aku melihat, dan aku melihat bahwa fulan bin fulan dibawa, dan fulan bin fulan, hingga aku menghitung 12 orang.' Terjadilah bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم telah mengutus sekelompok orang dalam sebuah misi sesaat sebelum itu. 'Mereka dibawa, dan mereka mengenakan pakaian yang kotor, dan urat-urat di sekitar leher mereka meneteskan darah. Dikatakan: bawa mereka ke Al-Baidakh — atau sungai Al-Baidakh. Mereka mencelupkan diri ke dalamnya, dan ketika mereka keluar, wajah-wajah mereka seperti bulan pada malam purnama. Kemudian kursi-kursi emas dibawa dan mereka duduk di atasnya. Kemudian sebuah nampan... dibawa; di dalamnya ada kurma muda, dan mereka makan darinya. Mereka tidak mengubah sisi-sisinya kecuali mereka makan dari buah yang mereka inginkan, dan aku makan bersama mereka.' Utusan dari kelompok itu (yang pergi dalam misi) datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, begini dan begitu kejadian yang kami alami. Si fulan terkena musibah (yaitu terbunuh), hingga dia menyebut dua belas orang yang sama yang disebutkan oleh wanita itu. Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, 'Bawa wanita itu kepadaku.' Dia datang dan beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, 'Ceritakan mimpimu kepada orang ini.' Dia menceritakannya, dan dia berkata, 'Itu seperti yang dia katakan, wahai Rasulullah.'" (Ahmad)

Sungai yang Bersinar di Pintu Surga

Ibnu 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Para syuhada berada di Sungai Baarig (secara harfiah, berkilauan) di pintu Surga, dalam sebuah kubah hijau. Rezeki mereka keluar kepada mereka di pagi dan sore hari." (Ahmad)

Berikut ini ada dalam hadis Al-Isrā (perjalanan malam), ketika Sidratul-Muntahaa disebutkan: "Dan dua sungai internal (atau tersembunyi) keluar dari sumbernya, dan dua sungai eksternal. Dua sungai internal ada di Surga, dan yang eksternal adalah Sungai Nil dan Sungai Eufrat."

Abu Buraizah رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Saihaan, Jaihaan, Eufrat, dan Nil — semuanya adalah dari sungai-sungai Surga." Ahmad dan Muslim meriwayatkan ini, dan

Ibnu 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Allah menurunkan

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 649

lima sungai dari Surga: Saihun, dan itu adalah sungai India; Jaihun, dan itu adalah sungai Balkh; Dijlah (Tigris) dan Eufrat, dan itu adalah sungai-sungai Iraq; dan Nil, yang merupakan sungai Mesir. Allah عز وجل menurunkan mereka dari satu sumber, dari sumber-sumber Surga — dari tingkatan terendahnya, di atas sayap-sayap Jibril. Dia menitipkan mereka di gunung-gunung dan membuat mereka mengalir di bumi. Dan Dia menempatkan banyak manfaat di dalamnya untuk orang-orang, dalam berbagai aspek kehidupan mereka. Dan itulah firman Allah عز وجل:

'Dan Kami menurunkan air dari langit (hujan) dalam ukuran (yang ditentukan), dan Kami menempatkannya di bumi.'" "Ketika Ya'juj dan Ma'juj keluar, Allah akan mengutus Jibril, dan dia akan mengangkat dari bumi: Al-Qur'an Al-'Azheem; semua ilmu; batu hitam, dari sudut Ka'bah, di Maqam Ibrahim; Tabut (Peti) Musa عليه السلام, beserta isinya; dan kelima sungai ini. Semua itu akan diangkat ke langit, dan itulah firman Allah عز وجل:
content_ar: أبو هريرة reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “Whoever it pleases that الله should give him wine to drink in الآخرة, then let him forsake it in the الدنيا. And whomever it pleases that الله should clothe him in silk in الآخرة, then let him forsake it in the الدنيا. The rivers of الجنة gush forth from underneath hills ~ or mountains ~ of musk (the best kind of perfume). Were the jewels (ornaments) of the lowest dweller of الجنة to be compared to the jewels (ornaments) of all the inhabitants of the الدنيا, the jewels (ornaments) of the lowest dweller of الجنة would be superior to the jewels (ornaments) of all inhabitants of the الدنيا (put together).” (Related by Tbn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) By الطريق of Abu Mu’aawiyah, ‘Abdullah # said, “The rivers of الجنة gush forth from musk (the best kind of perfume).” This is more authentic as a saying of ‘Abdullah than it is as a saying of النبي ﷺ #84. الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 645 A Description Of Al-Kauthar, The Most Famous of The Rivers Of الجنة. May الله 3¢ Give Us Drink From It, By His الفضل And الكرم “Verily, We have granted you (O محمد ﷺ 38) Al-Kauthar (a river in الجنة). لذلك turn in الصلاة to your Lord and sacrifice (to Him only). For he who hates you (O محمد ﷺ 2%), he will be cut of (from posterity and every الخير thing in this الدنيا 646 الكتاب of ‘fhe End: Great ‘rials and ‘Iribulations In another narration, أنس reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #6 said, “I have been given Al-Kauthar; it is a river that flows on the surface of the ground; its two sides are domes of pearls, without being roofed. I struck my hand on its soil, and its soil was musk (the best kind of perfume) Adhfar (pure, unmixed), and its pebbles are pearls.” (أحمد) أنس ibn Maalik 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% was asked about the Kauthar, and he 2% said, “It is river in الجنة which الله gave me. Its soil is musk (the best kind of perfume). Its water is whiter than milk and sweeter than honey. Birds, whose necks are like the necks of Al-Jazoor (camels that are ready to be slaughtered), come to drink from it” أبو بكر & said, “O Messenger of الله, they (the birds) are soft?” He #% said, “Eating them is softer than them.” (أحمد) Hudhaifah . related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 38 said, “Verily, in الجنة there are birds that are like Al-Bukhaatee (camels that have long necks).” أبو بكر % said, “They are soft, O Messenger of الله?” He #8 said, “The one who eats them is softer than them, and you are from those who will eat them, O أبو بكر.” (Related by AlHaakim) Al-Haakim then related it through Qataadah in Mursal form. The Narration Of Ibn “عمر الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 647 narration of this الحديث, and the latter said, “حسن صحيح.” The Narration Of Ibn ‘Abbaas Sa’eed ibn Jubair related that Ibn ‘Abbaas said about the Kauthar, “It is the goodness that الله. gave him (ie, gave to النبي ﷺ #8). (Bukhaaree) Tbn Bishr said, “I said to Sa’eed ibn Jubair, ‘Some people claim that it is a river in الجنة.” Sa’eed said, “The River that is in الجنة is from the goodness that الله gave to him (.¢., to النبي ﷺ ##).”” Ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Al-Kauthar is a river in الجنة; its two sides are gold and silver, and it flows on rubies and pearls. Its water is whiter than snow and sweeter than honey.” (Related by Ibn Jareer) Al’Aoufee related the same from Tbn ‘Abbaas + The Narration Of ‘عائشة % Abu ‘Ubaidah #: said, “I asked ‘عائشة % about the saying of الله “Verily, We have granted you (O محمد ﷺ #) Al-Kauthar (a river in الجنة).'"") She % said, “Al-Kauthar is a river which your النبي #3 was given. Its two shores are hollow pearls, and its vessels are, in number, like the stars.” (Bukhaaree) And ‘عائشة % said, “It is a river in الجنة. عندما one puts his fingers in his ears, he hears the flowing sound of that river.” In another narration, ‘عائشة % said, “Whoever would المحبة to hear the flowing sound of the water of Al-Kauthar, then he will not hear it with his eyes, but rather its sound is like the sound that is heard عندما a person puts two of his fingers in his ears.” (Related by Ibn Jareer) A Mention Of The Al-Baidakh River In الجنة ‘أنس 4 said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 liked the الخير dream, and maybe he would say, ‘Did any of you see a dream?’... A woman. 648 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations (حين) came and said, ‘O Messenger of الله, | saw what was like me entering الجنة, and I heard a sound, for which the people of الجنة cried. I looked, and I saw that لذا-and-لذا son of لذا-and-لذا was brought, and لذا-and-لذا-son of لذا-and-لذا, حتى I counted 12 men,’ It happened that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% had sent a group on a mission just قبل that. ‘They were brought, and upon them were dirty garments, and the veins around their necks were dripping blood. It was said: take them to Al-Baidakh — or the river of Al-Baidakh. They immersed themselves in it, and عندما they came out, their faces were like the moon on the night of the full moon. Then chairs of gold were brought and they sat on them. Then a tray... was brought; in it were unripe dates, and they ate from them. They did not change its sides except that they ate from the fruit that they wanted, and I ate with them,’ The herald of that group (that went on a mission) came and said, ‘O Messenger of الله, such and such were the matters that we went through. لذا and لذا was afflicted (ie., killed), حتى he counted the same twelve that the woman enumerated. الرسول ﷺ of الله #¥ said, ‘Bring the ‘woman to me.’ She came and he #& said, ‘Give an account of your dream to this one.’ She gave an account of it, and قال, ‘It is as sقال, O Messenger of الله.” (Atmad) A Shining River At The Gate Of الجنة Ibn ‘Abbaas . related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “The martyrs are on Baarig (literally, shining) river at the gate of الجنة, ina green dome, Their sustenance comes out to them in the morning and in the evening.” (أحمد) ‘The following is in the الحديث of Al-Israa (the night journey), عندما Sidratul-Muntahaa is being mentioned: “And two internal (or hidden) rivers are coming out of its source, and two external tivers. The two internal rivers are in الجنة, and the external ones are the Nile and the Euphrates.” Abu Buraizah . reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢ said, “Saihaan, Jaihaan, the Euphrates, and the Nile — these are all from the rivers of الجنة.” أحمد and مسلم related this, and the Jbn ‘Abbaas # reported that النبي ﷺ 4 said, “الله sent down الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 649 five rivers from الجنة: Saihoon, and it is the tiver of India; Jaihoon, and it is the river of Balkh; Dijlah and the Euphrates, and they are the rivers of Iraq; and the Nile, which is the river of Egypt. الله a sent them down from one source, from the sources of الجنة — from the lowest of their levels, on the wings of Jibreel. He deposited them in the mountains and made them flow in the earth. And He placed many benefits in them for the people, in the different aspects of their livelihood. And that is the saying of الله “And we sent down from the sky water (rain) in (due) measure, and We gave it lodging in the eartl at} “عندما Yajooj and Majooj come out, الله will send Jibreel, and he will raise the following from the earth: the Qus’an AlAdheem; all العلم; the black stone, from the corner of the House (Ka’bah), at the Station of Tbraaheem; the Taaboot (Ark) of Moosa, with that which is in it; and these five rivers. All of that will be raised to the sky, and that is the saying of الله #8:
